title: "I Love To Tell The Story" Romans 1:14-16

notes.introduction: A midweek Bible study sermon from Romans 1:14-16 in which the preacher examines the Christian's responsibility and motivation for sharing the gospel, using the hymn 'I Love to Tell the Story' as a framing device alongside a broader series on the doctrine of Christ.
outline: I. Recognize the Privilege: Paul's declaration of being a debtor (Romans 1:14) as a basis for understanding gospel witness as a God-given privilege
II. Make It a Priority: Paul's readiness to preach (Romans 1:15) and the pattern of purposeful, planned gospel proclamation seen in his ministry in Thessalonica and Athens (Acts 17)
III. Recognize the Power: Paul's declaration of no shame (Romans 1:16) grounded in the gospel as the power of God unto salvation, illustrated by Paul's own conversion in Acts 9
